svg.font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif
svg.text: WHEN ASKED: PROGRESS - DONE - CHALLENGES - FOLLOW THIS ARROW PATH
- Current phase: design / build / test (your real %)
- Proof: demo URL, screenshots, or repo link
Milestone ideas: discovery, MVP UI, API wiring,
We are building a responsive web app so students
can apply and industries can post & review roles.
Tip: quote 1 metric (e.g. screens done, API %).
Shipped vs planned: use DONE / WIP / NEXT when you speak.
- Student: profile/resume, listing + filters, apply, application list.
- Industry: post role (desc, requirements, deadline), applicant queue.
- Platform: email or in-app notify; admin visibility; responsive pages.
Green = student journey; purple = industry; gold = admin + messaging.
Use: problem, what you tried, outcome
- RBAC: routing, guards, tests so roles cannot cross.
- Data: resume storage, encryption, who can download.
- Notify: SMTP/API keys, queues, idempotent sends.
- UX: filter edge cases, skeletons, small breakpoints.
- Scope creep: defer nice-to-haves; log decisions.
- Team: merge conflicts, API contract drift - version endpoints.
We hit X, tried Y, result Z; next experiment is ...
Mention GDPR/CCPA awareness if you store EU/US user data.
Task doc: bridge students seeking internships with industries
posting roles - includes admin, comms, responsive UX, security.
Authoritative state: applications, messages, audit for admin.
What the spec asks for (add your notes verbally)

g.text: Registration + authentication for students and industries; enforce secure access on every route.
Profiles + resumes; browse/search listings; filters (location, industry, duration, etc.); apply; dashboard; status + notifications.
Profiles; publish internships (description, requirements, deadlines); review applications; contact + schedule interviews.
Secure admin panel; monitor activity; handle reported content and disputes.
Email notifications (application updates, interviews); in-app messaging between students and industries.
Intuitive UI; responsive layouts across device sizes.
Strong auth, encryption, access control; GDPR/CCPA-aware practices.

svg.text: Interview tip: pick 2-3 rows - data model + user flow + one trade-off.
Progress: where the product is vs. the spec (Intern Platform PDF).
Completed: concrete features merged or demoed; mention tests if any.
Challenges: technical + collaboration; pair with what you learned.
Finish strong: Next sprint: focus on X because it unblocks Y.
1) Our project is the Intern Platform - web app linking students
and companies (per task document): auth, listings, applications.
FREE / FREEMIUM RESOURCES - PRACTICAL WAYS TO BUILD (INTERN PLATFORM)
Student & internship-friendly stacks: use free tiers for demos; verify limits on each vendor site before production.
How to use for this project

g.text: Vercel, Netlify, GitHub Pages (static + serverless free tiers)
Deploy responsive student/industry UI; preview URLs for mentors.
Render, Railway, Fly.io (free/low tiers; watch sleep/cold start)
Host REST/GraphQL for auth, listings, applications, messaging hooks.
Neon, Supabase Postgres, PlanetScale (check current free limits)
Users, roles, internships, application rows, audit-friendly schema.
MongoDB Atlas M0; Supabase Storage; Cloudinary (media free tier)
Optional resume PDFs, company logos; keep PII access controlled.
Supabase Auth, Firebase Auth, Clerk hobby (MAU-limited)
Separate student vs industry flows; session/JWT for secure routes.
Resend, Brevo, Mailjet (free monthly sends - read fair-use)
Application status, interview invites; templated transactional mail.
GitHub: repo, Actions, Dependabot, secret scanning (public repos)
Collaborate, ship branches, block commits with leaked API keys.
Figma free; OpenAPI / Swagger / Postman; OWASP ZAP for basic scans
UX wireframes, API contract, quick security pass before demo.

svg.text: Security: .env local only; never commit keys - fits ethical hacking & secure SDLC.
Tamilselvan S - Ethical Hacker & Cybersecurity Specialist - SVG scales with viewport (width:100%, preserveAspectRatio)
